stories.headline: Iran-Israel war reignites: Israel strikes Iran on war's 100th day after Tehran's first missile barrage since April truce
stories.summary: Iran launched roughly 11 ballistic missiles at northern Israel late June 7 — its first direct attack since the April 8 ceasefire — after Israel bombed Beirut's Dahieh. Early June 8, the conflict's 100th day, Israel struck back at military targets in western and central Iran, with explosions reported near Isfahan, Tabriz and Tehran. Trump said he was 'not happy' about the Beirut strike and urged both sides back to negotiations.

perspectives.keyFraming: The renewed exchange exposes the limits of Trump's diplomacy as Israel acts unilaterally and Iran retaliates, undercutting claims a deal is near.
perspectives.summary: Iran fired several barrages toward Israel on June 7 for the first time since April, after Israel's Beirut strike killed two and wounded about 20, per Lebanon's health ministry. Israel struck back at targets in Iran early June 8. Trump said he was 'not happy' about the Beirut strike, reportedly told Netanyahu to hold off, and urged Tehran back to the negotiating table.

sources.title: Israel says it has struck Iran after taking missile fire
Israel says it struck targets in Iran after Tehran fires waves of missiles

perspectives.keyFraming: A fragile, repeatedly violated ceasefire has collapsed as Iran's first direct fire on Israel since April triggers Israeli strikes back on Iran.
perspectives.summary: Israel said Iran launched missiles in the first bombardment since the early-April ceasefire; Iran's state broadcaster confirmed the launches and warned attacks would continue unless Israel halted operations in Lebanon. Israel then struck military targets in western and central Iran early June 8. Trump pressed Iran to stop firing and return to talks.

sources.title: Israel says Iran launched a missile at it, in a first during fragile ceasefire
Israel says Iran launched missiles in first bombardment since fragile ceasefire

perspectives.keyFraming: Iran's barrages and threats against US targets show Tehran escalating, with Trump warning it to 'get back to the table.'
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning coverage framed Iran as unleashing missile barrages on Israel and threatening US targets in a sudden escalation, with Trump warning Tehran to return to negotiations and insisting a deal remains close, casting the president as pressing both sides to avoid a wider war.

sources.title: Iran unleashes missile barrages on Israel, threatens US targets in sudden escalation
Trump urges Iran to make a deal after Iran fires missiles at Israel

perspectives.keyFraming: On the war's 100th day the exchange is the most significant since April, with Israel striking back and Iran threatening to widen the conflict.
perspectives.summary: Coverage stressed the exchange was the most significant since the April pause, on the war's 100th day, with Iranian threats to block Bab al-Mandab and warnings from an aide to Iran's supreme leader. Israeli strikes reportedly hit a petrochemical facility in Khuzestan and targets near Isfahan, while the IDF said it intercepted Iran's missiles and ordered northern hospitals to protected wards.

sources.title: Iran, Houthis fire missiles at Israel as IDF strikes military targets in Iran
Iran Launches Ballistic Missile Attacks On Israel (Updated)

stories.headline: Magnitude 7.8 earthquake strikes off southern Philippines; tsunami warnings issued across the western Pacific
stories.summary: A powerful undersea earthquake — measured at magnitude 7.8, after initial reports of up to 8.2 — struck off Mindanao in the southern Philippines on June 7-8, prompting tsunami warnings for Philippine coasts and parts of Indonesia, Malaysia and Japan's Okinawa. A building reportedly collapsed in General Santos City and aftershocks of up to roughly magnitude 6.5 followed. Authorities urged coastal residents to move to higher ground.

perspectives.keyFraming: A major shallow quake triggers a fast-developing mass-casualty and tsunami-risk event across the western Pacific.
perspectives.summary: CNN and NPR reported the quake off Mindanao with tsunami warnings issued for the southern Philippines and neighboring coasts, forecast waves of up to about three meters for Philippine shores, a building collapse in General Santos City and strong aftershocks. Officials urged evacuations as the situation developed into the early hours.

sources.title: Philippines earthquake: 7.8 magnitude quake hits Mindanao, tsunami warning issued
7.8 magnitude quake hits southern Philippines; tsunami risk for some coasts

perspectives.keyFraming: Philippine outlets center the on-the-ground emergency response, evacuations and the school-day timing of the disaster.
perspectives.summary: GMA News ran live updates on the magnitude-7.8 quake and tsunami warning in Mindanao, detailing PHIVOLCS advisories, coastal evacuation orders and damage reports as residents fled to higher ground. The disaster struck as schools opened, raising concern about crowds in vulnerable areas.

sources.title: LIVE UPDATES: Magnitude 7.8 earthquake, tsunami warning in Mindanao

stories.headline: Oil turns higher as Iran-Israel war reignites; OPEC+ approves fourth symbolic output hike since Hormuz closure
stories.summary: Crude reversed its weekly slide as renewed Iran-Israel strikes on June 7-8 revived the war premium and dimmed hopes for a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, shut since late February. Brent ended the week up more than 4% near $94 and WTI near $91. On June 7 OPEC+ approved a fourth output-quota hike (+188,000 bpd for July), though the blockade makes most of the added barrels undeliverable. Weak Chinese demand had capped earlier gains.

perspectives.keyFraming: A brief diplomatic thaw pulled the war premium out of crude, but renewed strikes snapped prices back up and the Hormuz blockade keeps a floor under them.
perspectives.summary: Oil had retreated earlier in the week on diplomatic signals, but the June 7-8 missile exchange between Iran and Israel sent crude back up, with Brent finishing the week more than 4% higher. Analysts say the Hormuz blockade keeps a floor under prices and that sustained relief depends on traffic through the strait actually resuming.

sources.title: Current price of oil as of June 5, 2026
Opec agrees fourth monthly output rise despite Hormuz closure and price swings

perspectives.keyFraming: OPEC+ supply discipline and the Hormuz chokepoint mean energy prices stay elevated, and the new quota hike is symbolic while the strait stays shut.
perspectives.summary: The structural picture stays tight: the strait has been effectively closed since late February, so OPEC+'s fourth consecutive quota increase is largely cosmetic — delegates concede most of the added barrels cannot physically reach customers. Until shipping normalizes, US consumers face elevated pump prices regardless of diplomatic headlines.

sources.title: OPEC+ approves fourth oil output quota hike since Strait of Hormuz closure
Oil prices turn lower as U.S.-Iran ceasefire extension awaits Trump approval

stories.headline: Democrats press Senate to take up House-passed Iran war-powers resolution as the conflict reignites
stories.summary: The House's 215-208 June 3 passage of an Iran war-powers resolution pushed the fight to the Senate, where Minority Leader Schumer is pressing Leader Thune to allow a floor vote. The renewed missile exchange between Iran and Israel on June 7-8 — the war's 100th day — has hardened Democratic demands to rein in an open-ended conflict, even as any measure faces a likely Trump veto. A concurrent resolution would not require Trump's signature but generally lacks the force of law.

perspectives.keyFraming: Democrats argue Trump's 'war of choice' is unauthorized and failing, and vow to keep forcing votes.
perspectives.summary: Senate Democratic leadership framed the renewed push as building pressure to rein in an open-ended conflict, arguing the war raised gas prices, left Iran's nuclear program unverified and damaged US credibility, while insisting only a durable diplomatic settlement can end it.

sources.title: Schumer announces imminent vote on war powers resolution to rein in Trump's war in Iran
Schumer says Senate will vote on Iran war powers resolution next week
